moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the 20% single name cap.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged by the scanner and to manage overall exposure.
Increase QQQ to 25% to boost broad index exposure for stability.
Buy ANET at 5% as a top-quality name with a strong scanner setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation by cutting high-risk assets and favoring stable broad market exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect limited upside potential compared to other modes.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF to small size

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and align with the single name limit.
Sell SOXL entirely to remove a high-risk leveraged ETF, while trimming SPAL to 3% to keep some leveraged exposure within limits.
Trim QQQI to 15% due to its scanner flag and downtrend status.
Increase QQQ to 22% and CRWD to 15% to blend broad index with quality tech.
Buy ANET at 5% as a strong scanner setup to add quality tech exposure.
This plan focuses on risk-first growth, balancing broad market exposure with quality tech and limited leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while maintaining exposure to quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with reasonable growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, adhering to strict discipline.
Trim QQQI to 10% due to its scanner flag and downtrend status.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong uptrend in AI/Software.
Buy NOW at 10% and NVDU at 10% as high-scoring, uptrending scanner setups.
Buy TECS at 5% to add a leveraged ETF from the scanner list, within limits.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth. It leans into concentration and leverage, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and the potential for significant losses if stops are not strictly adhered to.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing low drawdown.
Sell SMH to exit a downtrending high-beta sector, aligning with income focus.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce exposure to high-beta single names.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs for steady distributions.
This plan aims to park cash for yield, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on yield and capital preservation. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and leveraged assets. While aiming for steady distributions, remember these are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
